g.text: Structured logs are queryable. Unstructured logs are a regex tax.
Same event, two encodings. The structure decides whether you debug in seconds or hours.
2024-03-15 14:23:01 ERROR Failed to process order 12345 for user abc-789: connection timeout to payment service
Log format changes -> every regex breaks silently
Multi-line stack trace? Embedded comma? Unicode? Each is a regex outage.
-> full-text scan across TB of text
-> parse every line, hope the regex still matches
WHERE level='ERROR' AND service='order-service' GROUP BY user_id, minute
Same event. 3 fragile regexes vs 9 indexed fields. Distributed systems demand the right side.
Unstructured falls apart at log volume - format drift breaks parsers, queries scale O(bytes), aggregations need ETL.
Structured turns logs into a queryable database: indexed fields, group-by aggregations, joinable on trace_id.
